proposals.title: Large multi-index data_loading: concurrent madvise across index files
proposals.detect: data_loading phase loads 2+ large pre-built mmap index files (each >50MB, e.g. pre_join_index at 1.73M×20B=35MB + payload pool). Sequential madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) of each file serializes I/O prefetch. Diagnostic: data_loading >150ms absolute with 2+ index files loaded.
proposals.fix: Launch concurrent madvise calls using #pragma omp parallel sections or std::async during data_loading phase — one section per large index file. Each section calls madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) on its own file range independently. Seen Q6 current run: data_loading=258ms with sub_adsh_index + pre_join_index loaded sequentially. Concurrent madvise expected to cut data_loading by 30-50% (to ~130-180ms) when index files are independent.

proposals.title: Update P16 threshold to include absolute >150ms trigger
proposals.fix: Previous threshold was '>40% hot runtime only'. Q6 data_loading=258ms (32% of 822ms) fell under the 40% ratio threshold but is a dominant bottleneck. Update to: 'data_loading >40% hot runtime OR >150ms absolute → apply zone-map-guided prefetch AND concurrent multi-index madvise (P27).'
proposals.current_text: P16: data_loading >40% hot runtime → apply zone-map-guided prefetch. [impact: 30-50%, freq: 3]

proposals.reason: All triggered in Q6 iter_1 plan correctness_checklist and data_structures sections; C9/C29/C15/C33 also triggered in Q2-Q4 agg structures and sort comparators.

meta.notes: Zero correctness failures. Q6 is architecturally SLOW at 822ms due to 34M-row scan + 300K groups + 2 pre-built index loads. All other queries in 34-153ms range.
